subject: Update on your mentor application
html:
Hi {{first_name}},

Thank you for wanting to become a mentor and help others grow. Unfortunately, after reviewing your application we've decided not to add your profile to the catalog this time.

We can't explain the decision in every individual case, but these are the most common reasons an application is declined:

  1. Consulting rather than mentoring. For example, profiles focused purely on career coaching services or on solving business problems for companies. OpenMentor is about person-to-person help within a shared competency: if you're an HR specialist, mentor other HR folks; if you're a marketer, mentor other marketers.
  2. Teaching instead of mentoring. Paid courses, training programs, group classes and the like. Mentoring is first of all about sharing your own experience, not about delivering a curriculum.
  3. Unethical offers. Resume fabrication, inflated experience, "guaranteed offer" packages and other grey-area job-hunting schemes.

Of course, we can be wrong. If you believe your profile was declined by mistake, email us at hello@openmentor.io and we'll take another look.

All the best,
The OpenMentor Team

This is an automated message from OpenMentor

https://openmentor.io
